Title: Successful user registration with valid credentials
Preconditions:
- System is live; PostgreSQL database is accessible.
- No duplicate email exists in the system.
Test Data:
- Email: `testuser@example.com`
- Password: `SecurePass123!`
- Confirm Password: `SecurePass123!`
- Full Name: `John Doe`
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the **Register activity** via the environment.
2. Enter the provided email, password, confirm password, and full name.
3. Click the **Submit** button.
Expected Result:
- System validates input (e.g., password match, valid email format).
- User account is created in PostgreSQL.
- Redirect to **Login activity** with a success message.
- Notification center receives a confirmation email (simulated or logged).

Title: Registration failure with invalid email format
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to **Register activity**.
2. Enter the invalid email and valid password/confirmation.
3. Click **Submit**.
Expected Result:
- System rejects registration with an error: *"Invalid email format."*
- No account is created in PostgreSQL.
- User remains on the registration page.

Title: Registration failure with duplicate email
Preconditions: - A user with email `duplicate@example.com` already exists in PostgreSQL.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to **Register activity**.
2. Enter the duplicate email and new password/confirmation.
3. Click **Submit**.
Expected Result:
- System displays: *"Email already registered."*
- No new account is created.
- User remains on the registration page.

Title: Successful login with valid credentials
Preconditions: - A user (`testuser@example.com`) exists in PostgreSQL with password `SecurePass123!`.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to **Login activity**.
2. Enter the valid email and password.
3. Click **Login**.
Expected Result:
- System authenticates the user.
- Redirects to **Profile** or **Notification center** (default post-login activity).
- Session is established (e.g., cookie set for environment tracking).

Title: Login failure with incorrect password
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to **Login activity**.
2. Enter the correct email and incorrect password.
3. Click **Login**.
Expected Result:
- System displays: *"Invalid email or password."*
- No session is created.
- User remains on the login page.

Title: Login failure with non-existent email
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to **Login activity**.
2. Enter the non-existent email and any password.
3. Click **Login**.
Expected Result:
- System displays: *"Invalid email or password."*
- No session is created.
- User remains on the login page.

Title: Successful newsletter subscription
Preconditions:
- User is logged in (`testuser@example.com`).
- Newsletter activity is accessible.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to **Newsletter activity** via the environment.
2. Enter the email in the subscription field.
3. Click **Subscribe**.
Expected Result:
- System validates the email format.
- Subscription is recorded in PostgreSQL (e.g., `newsletter_subscribers` table).
- Notification center sends a confirmation email (or logs the action).
- User sees: *"Successfully subscribed!"*

Title: Newsletter subscription failure with invalid email
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to **Newsletter activity**.
2. Enter the invalid email.
3. Click **Subscribe**.
Expected Result:
- System displays: *"Invalid email format."*
- No subscription is recorded in PostgreSQL.
- User remains on the newsletter page.

Title: Successful event creation by authenticated user
Preconditions:
- User `testuser@example.com` is logged in.
- **Create event** activity is accessible.
Test Data:
- Event Title: `Tech Conference 2023`
- Date: `2023-12-15`
- Time: `09:00 AM`
- Location: `Online`
- Description: `Annual tech conference with keynote speakers.`
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to **Create event** activity.
2. Fill in all fields with the provided data.
3. Click **Create Event**.
Expected Result:
- System validates required fields (title, date, time).
- Event is saved to PostgreSQL (e.g., `events` table) with creator ID linked to `testuser`.
- Notification center sends a confirmation to the creator (or logs it).
- User is redirected to **Join/View event** with a success message.

Title: Event creation failure with missing required field
Test Data:
- Event Title: *(left blank)*
- Date: `2023-12-15`
- Time: `09:00 AM`
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to **Create event**.
2. Leave the **Event Title** field empty but fill in other fields.
3. Click **Create Event**.
Expected Result:
- System displays: *"Event title is required."*
- No event is created in PostgreSQL.
- User remains on the event creation page.

Title: Successful event joining by authenticated user
Preconditions:
- An event (`Tech Conference 2023`) exists in PostgreSQL.
- User `testuser@example.com` is logged in.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to **Join/View event** activity.
2. Search for and select the event.
3. Click **Join Event**.
Expected Result:
- System records the user-event association in PostgreSQL (e.g., `event_participants` table).
- Notification center sends a confirmation to the user (or logs it).
- User sees: *"You have joined Tech Conference 2023."*
- Event appears in the user’s **Profile** activity under "Joined Events."

Title: Attempt to join an event already joined by the user
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to **Join/View event**.
2. Select the same event and click **Join Event**.
Expected Result:
- System displays: *"You have already joined this event."*
- No duplicate entry is created in PostgreSQL.
- User remains on the event page.

Title: View user profile with joined events
Preconditions:
- User `testuser@example.com` is logged in.
- User has joined `Tech Conference 2023`.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to **Profile** activity.
2. Verify the displayed information:
   - Full Name: `John Doe`
   - Email: `testuser@example.com`
   - Joined Events: `Tech Conference 2023`
Expected Result:
- All profile data matches the PostgreSQL records.
- No errors or missing fields are displayed.

Title: Edit user profile with valid data
Test Data:
- New Full Name: `Johnathan Doe`
- New Email: `john.doe@example.com` (previously unused)
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to **Profile**.
2. Click **Edit Profile**.
3. Update the full name and email with the provided data.
4. Click **Save Changes**.
Expected Result:
- System validates the new email format and uniqueness.
- Profile is updated in PostgreSQL.
- User sees: *"Profile updated successfully."*
- Changes are reflected in subsequent **Profile** views.

Title: Profile update failure with duplicate email
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to **Profile**.
2. Click **Edit Profile**.
3. Change email to `otheruser@example.com`.
4. Click **Save Changes**.
Expected Result:
- System displays: *"Email is already in use."*
- No changes are saved to PostgreSQL.
- User remains on the edit profile page.

Title: Receive notification for joined event
Preconditions:
- User `testuser@example.com` has joined `Tech Conference 2023`.
- Notification center is configured to send join confirmations.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to **Notification center**.
2. Verify the presence of a notification:
   - Title: *"You joined Tech Conference 2023"*
   - Timestamp: Current date/time.
Expected Result:
- Notification is displayed with correct details.
- Notification is marked as "Unread" until viewed.
- Notification is linked to the event in **Join/View event**.

Title: Successful user logout
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to **Exit** activity (e.g., via a logout button in the environment).
2. Confirm logout if prompted.
Expected Result:
- Session is terminated (cookies cleared, session ID invalidated).
- User is redirected to the **Login activity**.
- User sees: *"You have been logged out."*
- Attempting to access **Profile** or **Notification center** redirects to login.

Title: Display flash message after event creation
Preconditions:
- User has created an event (`Tech Conference 2023`).
- Flash activity is configured to show success messages.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to **Join/View event** after event creation.
2. Observe the flash message at the top of the page.
Expected Result:
- Flash message displays: *"Event created successfully!"*
- Message disappears after 5 seconds (or on page refresh).
- No functional impact on other activities.

Title: Verify PostgreSQL data consistency after registration
